architecture_summary: Node/TypeScript worker. Google Form Apps Script calls a shared-secret webhook, Google Sheets is queue/state, Gemini drafts a response from repo-local RAG markdown, Discord receives human approval/edit actions, and Gmail sends approved replies. Main trust boundaries are public webhook input, Discord interactions, Google OAuth credentials, Gemini API key, CI/CD deploy secrets, and GitHub Actions supply chain.

infrastructure.secret_management: GitHub Actions secrets plus server .env generated from APP_ENV

findings.title: Mutable third-party GitHub Actions run in secret-bearing deploy workflow
findings.description: The deploy workflow references third-party Actions by mutable version tags instead of immutable commit SHAs: docker/login-action@v3, docker/setup-buildx-action@v3, docker/build-push-action@v6, appleboy/ssh-action@v1.0.3, and appleboy/scp-action@v0.1.7. These steps run in a workflow with package write permission and deployment secrets.
findings.exploit_scenario: 1. An attacker compromises an upstream action publisher account or moves a mutable action tag. 2. A normal push to main triggers this workflow. 3. The compromised action code runs in CI with GITHUB_TOKEN package write access and deploy secrets such as SERVER_SSH_KEY, GHCR_READ_TOKEN, and APP_ENV available to later deploy steps. 4. The attacker exfiltrates secrets, pushes a malicious image, or alters production deployment behavior.
findings.impact: Compromise of CI/CD could expose production deployment credentials, registry credentials, and application environment secrets, then allow malicious image publication or unauthorized production deployment changes.
findings.recommendation: Pin third-party actions to reviewed commit SHAs and periodically update them through a controlled dependency-update process. Keep first-party actions at least version-pinned; prioritize SHA pinning for all non-actions/* third-party actions used in jobs with secrets.
findings.verification: independently verified by security-reviewer subagent; self-confirmed against workflow permissions, trigger, and secret-bearing deploy steps

npm_audit.note: Moderate uuid advisory reaches googleapis through googleapis-common/gaxios; below daily CSO reporting threshold and fix requires googleapis semver-major upgrade.

non_reportable_notes: Dockerfile lacks a USER directive and compose does not override user, so the production container likely runs as root. Independent verification scored this 3/10 for daily reporting because no privileged mode, cap_add, host mounts, or concrete standalone exploit path were found.

docker-compose.yml healthcheck calls /health, but the HTTP server only implements /webhooks/google-form-submit and 404 for other paths. This is an operational deployment risk, not a CSO daily security finding.
No .gitleaks.toml or .secretlintrc was found. This is a recommended control, not a daily finding.
Global user-installed AI coding skills were not scanned; repo-local .agents skills were scanned only.
